# bayesroc

Bayesian signal detection models in R, with a formula-based API.
Compiles models to Stan (via
[`cmdstanr`](https://mc-stan.org/cmdstanr/)) or
[`NumPyro`](https://github.com/pyro-ppl/numpyro) and supports multiple
univariate and bivariate model families with fixed and random effects on
any model parameter.

## Installation

bayesroc is not yet on CRAN. Install from GitHub:

``` r

# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("jed709/bayesroc")
```

The Stan backend requires `cmdstanr` and a working CmdStan installation:

``` r

install.packages("cmdstanr",
  repos = c("https://stan-dev.r-universe.dev", getOption("repos")))
cmdstanr::install_cmdstan()
```

The optional JAX backend runs in a managed Python virtual environment.
It requires a **system Python (\>= 3.10) installed first** — bayesroc
creates the venv but does not install Python itself. On macOS/Linux a
`python.org`, Homebrew (`brew install python`), pyenv, or conda
interpreter all work and are auto-discovered. On Windows, install Python
from [python.org](https://www.python.org/downloads/) rather than the
Microsoft Store (the Store “python” stub is not a usable interpreter).
Then:

``` r

bayesroc::install_jax_backend()   # creates a managed venv with jax + numpyro
bayesroc::check_jax_backend()     # confirms the backend is ready ("ok")
```

Once installed, switch backends with `fit_broc(model, backend = "jax")`.

If
[`install_jax_backend()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/install_jax_backend.md)
cannot find Python, point it at one explicitly with
`install_jax_backend(python = "/path/to/python")`, or set the
interpreter used for fitting with
[`set_jax_python()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/set_jax_python.md).
See
[`?install_jax_backend`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/install_jax_backend.md)
and
[`?check_jax_backend`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/check_jax_backend.md)
for troubleshooting.

## A simple example

Formulas provided to univariate bayesroc models must supply both a
response variable and a binary indicator variable using either 0/1 or
-0.5/0.5 coding that denotes whether the trial is a lure or a target.
These variables should be provided in the form
`response | is_old ~ predictors`, where this formula implictly applies
to d’ and subsequent formulae are provided for other model parameters
(e.g., `criterion ~ predictors`, `sigma ~ predictors`).

The code below shows how a hierarchical unequal variance signal
detection model (UVSD) can be fit to the bundled `prm09` dataset (97
subjects, 480 items, 6-point confidence; Pratte et al., 2010), with
correlated subject- and item-level random intercepts on every parameter
that are cross-correlated between d’ and sigma. We then view the model
summary and plot the model-implied ROC curve. Because the dataset is
very large, we fit this basic example to the first 20 participants.
Here, `resp` is the rating response on a 6-point scale (with higher
responses indicating “sure old”) and `cond` is the 0/1 coded indicator
variable.

``` r

library(bayesroc)
data(prm09)

first20 <- prm09[prm09$sub<20,]

m <- broc(brf(resp | cond ~ 1 + (1|s|sub) + (1|i|item),
              criterion ~ 1 + (1|sub) + (1|item),
              sigma ~ 1 + (1|s|sub) + (1|i|item),
              family = uvsd()),
          data = first20)
fit <- fit_broc(m, backend = "stan")

summary(fit)
plot_roc_curve(fit)
```

If we are not interested in modeling trial-level data (e.g., random
effects on items), we can aggregate the data to per-subject counts and
fit a quick version of the model.

``` r

agg <- as.data.frame(table(first20[c("sub", "cond", "resp")]), 
                     responseName = "count")

m <- broc(brf(resp | cond ~ 1 + (1|s|sub),
              criterion ~ 1 + (1|sub),
              sigma ~ 1 + (1|s|sub),
              family = uvsd()),
              counts = "count",
          data = agg)
fit <- fit_broc(m, backend = "stan")
```

See the
[vignette](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/vignettes/univariate-recognition.Rmd)
for an in-depth example.

### Backends and speed

The Stan backend uses a fused likelihood with analytic gradients by
default (`batch_likelihood = TRUE`), which is much faster than plain
Stan with autodiff and needs no extra toolchain beyond `cmdstanr`. Pass
`batch_likelihood = FALSE` to force the plain path (e.g. for
cross-checking). The Stan backend is single-threaded by default but
supports multithreading via `reduce_sum` by passing `threads = TRUE` to
[`broc()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/broc.md) and
specifying `threads_per_chain = N_threads` when calling
[`fit_broc()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/fit_broc.md).

The NumPyro backend (`fit_broc(m, backend = "jax")`) is generally faster
than Stan and uses analytic custom JVPs. This backend only supports
multithreading and the number of threads spawned for each chain depends
on the model family, fixed- and random- effects structure, and dataset
size. Threads per chain cannot be set directly. At present, the NumPyro
backend is CPU-only, but GPU support may be added in future releases.

## Supported families

| Family | Description |
|----|----|
| [`evsd()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/evsd.md) | Equal variance signal detection model |
| [`uvsd()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/uvsd.md) | Unequal variance signal detection model |
| [`dpsd()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/dpsd.md) | Dual process signal detection model (Yonelinas, 1994) |
| [`mixture()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/mixture.md) | Mixture signal detection model (with optional lure mixture; DeCarlo, 2002) |
| [`source_mixture()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/source_mixture.md) | Source-discrimination mixture (DeCarlo, 2003a) |
| [`bivariate_gaussian()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/bivariate_gaussian.md) | Bivariate Gaussian signal detection model (DeCarlo, 2003b; Starns et al., 2014) |
| [`bivariate_dp()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/bivariate_dp.md) | Bivariate dual process signal detection model (Starns et al., 2014) |
| [`vrdp2d()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/vrdp2d.md) | 2D variable recollection dual process model (Onyper et al., 2010) |
| [`cdp()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/cdp.md) | Continuous dual process model for Remember/Know (Wixted & Mickes, 2010) |
| [`cumulative()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/cumulative.md) | Plain cumulative ordinal regression |
| [`bivariate_cumulative()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/bivariate_cumulative.md) | Bivariate cumulative ordinal regression |

## Bundled datasets

bayesroc ships five datasets:

| Dataset | Format | Use |
|----|----|----|
| `prm09` | trial-level, 46,495 × 5 (97 subjects, 480 items, 6-point confidence) | Pratte et al. (2010) — hierarchical univariate models |
| `whitridge_2024` | trial-level, 7,560 × 6 (42 subjects, 360 items, 6-point confidence) | Whitridge et al. (2024) — hierarchical univariate models with within-subject effects |
| `yonelinas_1999` | aggregated counts, 108 × 4 (3 item types × 6×6 detect/source ratings) | Yonelinas (1999) — item/source judgements for bivariate SDT models (`bivariate_gaussian`, `bivariate_dp`, `vrdp2d`) |
| `hilford_2002` | aggregated counts, 12 × 3 (2 sources × 6 ratings) | Hilford et al. (2002) — [`source_mixture()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/source_mixture.md) models |
| `rotello_2005` | aggregated counts, 44 × 5 | Rotello et al. (2005) — Remember/Know data for [`cdp()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/cdp.md) |

``` r

data(prm09)
data(whitridge_2024)
data(yonelinas_1999)
data(hilford_2002)
data(rotello_2005)
```

See the corresponding help pages
(e.g. [`?yonelinas_1999`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/yonelinas_1999.md),
[`?hilford_2002`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/hilford_2002.md))
for column descriptions.

## Documentation

- [`?broc`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/broc.md) — main
  entry point
- [`?brf`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/brf.md) — formula
  composition
- [`?fit_broc`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/fit_broc.md)
  — fitting (Stan or JAX)
- `summary(fit)`, `plot(fit)`, `predict(fit)`, `pp_check(fit)`,
  `loo(fit)`, `posterior_epred(fit)`, `posterior_linpred(fit)` — S3
  methods on the fitted model
- [`?plot_roc_curve`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/plot_roc_curve.md)
  — ROC curve plot
- [`?conditional_smooths`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/conditional_smooths.md)
  — smooth-term evaluation/plotting (mgcv `s()` / `t2()` are supported
  in any parameter formula)
- [`?broc_prior`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/broc_prior.md)
  — prior specification

## License

MIT. See `LICENSE.md`.

# Package index

## All functions

- [`bivariate_cumulative()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/bivariate_cumulative.md)
  : Bivariate Cumulative Ordinal Model
- [`bivariate_dp()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/bivariate_dp.md)
  : Bivariate Dual Process Signal Detection Model
- [`bivariate_gaussian()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/bivariate_gaussian.md)
  : Bivariate Gaussian Signal Detection Model
- [`brf()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/brf.md) :
  Compose a Multi-Parameter SDT Model Formula
- [`broc()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/broc.md) :
  Specify a Bayesian SDT Model
- [`broc_prior()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/broc_prior.md)
  : Set a Prior on an SDT Model Parameter
- [`c(`*`<broc_prior>`*`)`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/c.broc_prior.md)
  : Combine Multiple Priors
- [`cdp()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/cdp.md) :
  Continuous Dual Process Signal Detection Model
- [`check_jax_backend()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/check_jax_backend.md)
  : Diagnose the JAX backend setup
- [`conditional_smooths()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/conditional_smooths.md)
  : Conditional Smooth Effects
- [`cumulative()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/cumulative.md)
  : Cumulative Ordinal Regression Model
- [`dpsd()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/dpsd.md) : Dual
  Process Signal Detection Model
- [`evsd()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/evsd.md) :
  Equal Variance Signal Detection Model
- [`fit_broc()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/fit_broc.md)
  : Compile and Fit a Bayesian ROC Model
- [`get_broc_prior()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/get_broc_prior.md)
  : Preview the Priors for an SDT Model
- [`get_numpyro_config()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/get_numpyro_config.md)
  : Extract the Generated NumPyro (JAX) Config
- [`get_stan_code()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/get_stan_code.md)
  : Extract Generated Stan Code
- [`get_stan_data()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/get_stan_data.md)
  : Extract Stan Data List
- [`hilford_2002`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/hilford_2002.md)
  : Hilford et al. (2002) source-discrimination data
- [`install_jax_backend()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/install_jax_backend.md)
  : Install the JAX/NumPyro backend into a managed venv
- [`jax_python()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/jax_python.md)
  : Resolve the Python executable used by the JAX backend
- [`link_logit()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/link_logit.md)
  : Logistic Link Function
- [`link_probit()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/link_probit.md)
  : Probit Link Function (default)
- [`load_broc_fit()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/load_broc_fit.md)
  : Load a previously-saved broc_fit
- [`loo(`*`<broc_fit>`*`)`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/loo.broc_fit.md)
  : LOO-CV for Fitted SDT Models
- [`mixture()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/mixture.md)
  : Mixture Signal Detection Model
- [`plot(`*`<broc_fit>`*`)`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/plot.broc_fit.md)
  : Plot Model Parameters
- [`plot_roc_curve()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/plot_roc_curve.md)
  : Plot Model-Implied ROC Curves
- [`posterior_epred(`*`<broc_fit>`*`)`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/posterior_epred.broc_fit.md)
  : Posterior expected response value
- [`posterior_linpred(`*`<broc_fit>`*`)`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/posterior_linpred.broc_fit.md)
  : Posterior draws of a linear predictor (dpar)
- [`pp_check(`*`<broc_fit>`*`)`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/pp_check.broc_fit.md)
  : Posterior Predictive Check for SDT Models
- [`predict(`*`<broc_fit>`*`)`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/predict.broc_fit.md)
  : Posterior Predictions from a Fitted Bayesian ROC Model
- [`print(`*`<brf>`*`)`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/print.brf.md)
  : Print method for brf objects
- [`print(`*`<broc_family>`*`)`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/print.broc_family.md)
  : Print method for a bayesroc family
- [`print(`*`<broc_link>`*`)`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/print.broc_link.md)
  : Print method for a bayesroc link
- [`print(`*`<broc_model>`*`)`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/print.broc_model.md)
  : Print Method for SDT Model
- [`print(`*`<broc_prior>`*`)`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/print.broc_prior.md)
  : Print Method for Prior
- [`print(`*`<broc_prior_list>`*`)`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/print.broc_prior_list.md)
  : Print Method for Prior List
- [`print(`*`<broc_prior_table>`*`)`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/print.broc_prior_table.md)
  : Print method for get_broc_prior output
- [`print(`*`<prior_lookup>`*`)`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/print.prior_lookup.md)
  : Print Method for Prior Lookup
- [`prm09`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/prm09.md) :
  Pratte et al. (2010) word recognition memory data
- [`resp()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/resp.md) :
  resp() – Multi-response marker for bivariate/CDP models
- [`rotello_2005`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/rotello_2005.md)
  : Rotello et al. (2005) Remember/Know data
- [`save_broc_fit()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/save_broc_fit.md)
  : Save a fitted broc_fit to disk
- [`set_jax_python()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/set_jax_python.md)
  : Override the Python executable used by the JAX backend
- [`source_mixture()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/source_mixture.md)
  : Source Mixture Signal Detection Model
- [`summary(`*`<broc_fit>`*`)`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/summary.broc_fit.md)
  : Summarize a Fitted SDT Model
- [`uvsd()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/uvsd.md) :
  Unequal Variance Signal Detection Model
- [`vrdp2d()`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/vrdp2d.md) :
  Two-Dimensional Variable Recollection Dual Process Model
- [`whitridge_2024`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/whitridge_2024.md)
  : Whitridge et al. (2024) recognition memory data
- [`yonelinas_1999`](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/reference/yonelinas_1999.md)
  : Yonelinas (1999) source-memory data

# Articles

### All vignettes

- [Analyzing univariate recognition data with
  bayesroc](https://jed709.github.io/bayesroc/articles/univariate-recognition.md):
